mentions.canonical_name: diabetic foot ulcer (right lateral dorsal foot wound)

attributes.body_site: right foot (dorsal aspect of the lateral right foot, just proximal to the fifth MTP joint)
attributes.notes: Started as a blister that patient picked at; blister became red and infected. Patient reports increased drainage lately. Exam: 1x2 cm circular wound with yellow slough and minimal granulation tissue; no surrounding erythema/cellulitis, no odor, no necrosis, no bony exposure; x-ray showed no evidence of osteomyelitis.
Blood sugars still in the 200s; last A1c about 10.
Antibiotics previously seemed to help but did not make the foot ulcer go away.
Doctor notes patient does not have any fever and vitals look good.
Plan to get patient a surgical shoe to offload pressure from the foot ulcer; patient has been wearing regular shoes.
Visit for nonhealing foot ulcer in the setting of type 2 diabetes.
Foot wound started as a blister that patient picked at; became red and infected.
Started as a little blister; patient picked at it and it became red and infected. No trauma reported.
Blister became a little bit red after being picked at.
